slides.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
slides.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
slides.speaker_notes: Export kind: policy_contract_comparison_deck. Deck family: comparison. Sources: structured_result, document_agent_comparison.

slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
The Access Review Evidence Log is a synthetic test document designed to validate access review processes, with control closure dependent on verifying incomplete evidence items such as EV-002 and EV-003, whereas the Privileged Account Approval Email establishes a conditional, governance-driven approval for specific personnel tied to strict usage controls and mandatory revalidation upon staff changes.  
- The Access Review Evidence Log is explicitly synthetic and not real audit evidence, used solely for process testing.  
- Control closure in the Access Review Evidence Log requires verification of missing or in-progress evidence items, unlike the Privileged Account Approval Email’s focus on procedural compliance.  
- The Privileged Account Approval Email mandates revalidation upon personnel changes, emphasizing accountability, while the Access Review Evidence Log does not address personnel changes.  
- The Access Review Evidence Log centers on evidence completeness and provenance verification, whereas the Privileged Account Approval Email emphasizes usage limits, logging, and quarterly reviews.  
- The Access Review Evidence Log involves five evidence items with gaps, while the Privileged Account Approval Email governs two administrators under defined governance terms.  
- The Access Review Evidence Log is a controlled test artifact, while the Privileged Account Approval Email is an operational governance document requiring ongoing compliance.
